title: Delivered JavaScript, not the specified TypeScript
evidence: stack.json sets language=typescript, but the workspace contains only .js files (server.js, test.js, demo.js) with no .ts sources, no tsconfig.json, and no type annotations; scores.json records idiomatic=0.0 and maintainability=0.20 as a result.
suggestion: Implement in TypeScript (typed Express handlers, tsconfig.json, ts-jest) to satisfy the language factor for this cell.

title: SQLite used but in-memory only — data not persisted
evidence: server.js:10 `new Database(':memory:')` — the SQLite engine is used, but in-memory mode discards all data on process exit; R7's how_to_verify asks for persistence 'not just in-memory state'.
suggestion: Open a file-backed database (e.g. new Database('books.db')) so records survive restarts.

title: POST/PUT echo raw `year` instead of the persisted value
evidence: server.js:58 and server.js:159 return `year` from the request body (undefined when omitted) rather than the stored `year || null` written at server.js:40/135, so the create/update response can differ from the row in the DB.
suggestion: Return `year: year || null` (or re-read the row) so the response matches persisted state.
